steps.title: Rewrite as a quadratic
steps.content: Expand the equation and move everything to one side: $$x(kx-36)=-9$$ $$kx^2-36x+9=0.$$

steps.title: Use the discriminant shortcut
steps.content: Two distinct real solutions means the discriminant is positive. With $a=k$, $b=-36$, and $c=9$, use $$(-36)^2-4(k)(9)\gt 0.$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: The greatest integer less than $36$ is $35$. Check: $1296-36(35)=36\gt 0$, so the quadratic still has two distinct real solutions. The answer is 35.
